checks.a_analysis: The output contains 25 sections corresponding to the 25 pages of the source PDF. The text in each section accurately reflects the content of the corresponding slide as seen in the source and the provided OCR. There are no invented section headings, fabricated body text, or hallucinated introductions/conclusions. The text 'Microsoft Word Logo - PNG and Vector - Logo Download' appearing in several sections corresponds to metadata associated with the Word icon in the source, which was captured in the OCR and correctly transcribed by the pipeline.

The output contains the email address 'Josh.Meredith@microsoft.com' on page 2 and the URL 'https:///www.microsoft365.com/chat' on page 8, both of which are present in the source PDF (including the typo of the triple slash in the URL). The URL 'https://www.microsoft365.com/launch/word' appearing in several practice sections also matches the source text. No fabricated contact information was found.

The source PDF contains 25 slides. The output contains 25 sections, each representing one of the slides. All content from the slides, including the 'Demo', 'Creating New Content', and 'Questions' slides, is represented in the output.

The source document contains no formal data tables. It contains text-based grids (e.g., Slide 4 and Slide 24), which have been converted into structured text and lists in the output without any loss of informational rows or items.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All text elements from the slides are present in the output. For example, on Slide 24, all 12 prompt examples from the grid are included. On Slide 4, all four 'Key Elements' boxes are fully transcribed with their respective headings, sub-headings, and descriptions. No significant omissions were identified.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output follows the source PDF closely, preserving specific formatting choices or errors found in the source (such as '3key points' without a space on Slide 24 and the triple slash in the URL on Slide 8). No changes were found that alter the meaning of the document.

checks.a_analysis: The output contains sections corresponding to each slide in the source PDF. Some slide titles are identical in the source (e.g., 'Art of Prompting - Key Elements' on pages 4 and 5), and the output correctly preserves these as distinct sections. There are no instances where a single block of text from the source is repeated multiple times in the output within its context when it only appears once in the original.

The document uses H2 for slide titles and H3 for sub-sections within slides (e.g., on pages 6, 7, and 24). This hierarchy is consistent and logical for a slide-based document. No levels are skipped (e.g., jumping from H2 to H4).

The source document does not contain any formal tables. It uses grid layouts which the output has represented using headings, lists, and paragraphs. The JSON correctly reports 0 tables as structural elements.
The output follows the logical slide-by-slide sequence of the source PDF. The information flow is preserved correctly from introduction to practice exercises.

Lists throughout the document, including nested lists on pages 6 and 7, are correctly identified and structured as ordered or unordered lists in the JSON output, preserving the original grouping of points.

There are several issues with links in the conversion. First, explicit URLs appearing in the source text on pages 8, 12, 13, 14, and 18 (e.g., 'https://www.microsoft365.com/launch/word') are rendered as plain text segments with underline formatting in the output, but lack semantic 'href' attributes, meaning they are not clickable link objects. Second, on page 9, a link was injected into the word 'Word' pointing to 'https://www.microsoft365.com/launch/word', which does not exist as a link in that sentence in the source text.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF pages 8, 12, 13, 14, 18 contain explicit URLs. Page 9 text 'You can also use Copilot in Word...'
checks.b_evidence_output: JSON elements for slides 12, 13, 14, and 18 show URLs as text only. Slide 9 (Index 8) shows a link object for 'Word': 'href': 'https://www.microsoft365.com/launch/word'

checks.a_analysis: The conversion from a PowerPoint-style presentation to a linearized HTML document is handled appropriately. The visual organization of information remains consistent with the source intent. Content from multi-column slides (e.g., Page 4 and Page 24) has been successfully transformed into a single-column layout suitable for web viewing without loss of meaning or clarity.

The source document does not contain any formal data tables. Content that is visually arranged in a grid-like fashion in the PDF (such as the prompt examples on Page 24) is rendered as standard text blocks in the HTML, which is a correct representation for this format. No broken or misaligned tables were observed.

Several meaningful images are missing or incorrectly rendered in the HTML output. In the Slide 2 section, the headshot of Josh Meredith, the group photo in the kitchen, and the jetboarding photo are missing, with a large green rectangular block appearing in the layout instead. In the Slide 10 section ('Demo'), the Copilot logo and the word 'Demo' are missing, replaced by a green block. Additionally, in the 'Copilot Word Prompts' section corresponding to Page 24, all 12 Microsoft Word icons are missing and are instead represented by the descriptive text 'Microsoft Word Logo - PNG and Vector - Logo Download'.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2 contains photos of Josh Meredith (headshot), family in kitchen, and Josh jetboarding. Page 10 shows 'Demo' with the Copilot logo. Page 24 contains 12 Microsoft Word icons.
checks.b_evidence_output: Slide 2 section is missing the headshot and other photos, showing a green block. Slide 10 shows only a green block. 'Copilot Word Prompts' section shows text 'Microsoft Word Logo...' instead of icons.
checks.b_evidence_location: Slide 2, Slide 10, and 'Copilot Word Prompts' section (Page 24)

checks.a_analysis: No genuine body content (paragraphs, lists, or tables) is duplicated in the HTML output. Slide titles appearing as section navigation landmarks as well as body headings is an intentional accessibility feature and is not considered content duplication.
